title: 'Total disgrace': Argentina branded 'classless' as post-final conduct sparks outrage
content: Argentina faced criticism for their behavior after losing the World Cup final.  Players did not remain on the podium during Spain's trophy presentation.  This action drew condemnation from many football community members.  Earlier, a confrontation occurred between players from both teams on the field.  The team's overall tournament performance also faced scrutiny from some observers.

title: 'I will continue my fast': Wangchuk says peaceful protesters dealt with brutality
content: Climate activist Sonam Wangchuk continues his hunger strike after witnessing police actions against protestors. He demands that youth leaders meet parliamentarians or he be allowed to meet them. Wangchuk appeals to the government and police to let students present their grievances. Thousands marched to Parliament demanding the Education Minister's resignation over examination irregularities. Police used tear gas and force, detaining several protestors during the march.

title: JPMorgan made 40% AI job cuts in some teams, but CEO is not ‘convinced’ of its results
content: JPMorgan Chase has reduced some jobs by up to forty percent using artificial intelligence.  CEO Jamie Dimon believes AI offers no unique competitive advantage for the bank.  He stated that AI will not significantly increase the bank's profit margins.  The bank is increasing recruitment for AI-related roles while hiring fewer bankers.  JPMorgan Chase reported strong net income and investment banking fees for the second quarter.

title: 'Messi, Messi!': Argentina fans honour their captain as Spain lift FIFA World Cup trophy
content: Spain defeated Argentina 1-0 in the FIFA World Cup 2026 final after extra time. Lionel Messi's final World Cup match ended in heartbreak for the legendary player. Thousands of Argentine fans showed unwavering support for Messi despite the disappointing loss. Spain's disciplined performance denied Messi opportunities to influence the crucial match. The defeat concluded Argentina's bid to defend their World Cup title successfully.

title: No waiver, no worries: India double downs on Russian oil in July
content: Industry experts say India's crude procurement pattern during the US-Iran conflict reflects a flexible and well-diversified sourcing strategy. India’s crude oil imports from Russia in July so far have averaged at around 2.45 million barrels per day.

title: Airbnb CEO says will keep investing in New York despite Mamdani's rent freeze plan
content: Airbnb and Anthropic are expanding their New York City operations significantly.  This investment occurs despite concerns from business leaders about city policies.  Airbnb purchased a building for its growing New York workforce.  Anthropic will lease a large office building and double its staff.  These expansions highlight continued business activity amidst policy debates.

title: 26-year-old quits Chicago corporate job for $19.25-an-hour Yellowstone role
content: Zoe De La Paz, 26, left her corporate engineering job in Chicago to work as a seamstress at Yellowstone National Park, turning a high-school hobby into a full-time career. After gaining experience through backstage jobs at music venues, she now repairs uniforms at Yellowstone while working towards her long-term goal of joining Broadway and film costume departments in New York City.

title: Couple buys dream home, discovers it is a snake habitat after moving in
content: Tyler and Lauren Bouldin bought an abandoned historic Pennsylvania home in 2020.  They discovered the neglected grounds had become a thriving habitat for various snakes.  The couple learned to manage the wildlife by making the property less attractive to them.  They safely captured and relocated snakes encountered near their home.  Five years later, the property is transformed into their dream family home.

title: US judge to Meta's laid off employees: Cannot stop the company from job cuts
content: A US judge has allowed Meta Platforms to proceed with planned layoffs of 26 employees. These employees had sued the company alleging AI-driven discrimination in job cuts. The judge declined an emergency request to halt the layoffs while claims proceed. He stated the court may reconsider if new evidence emerges about AI usage. Meta denies wrongdoing and states human decisions guided the workforce reduction.

title: Vacant for decades? Why the government can't simply take over abandoned properties
content: In India, the absence of a comprehensive law governing abandoned buildings leads to fragmented regulations across various legislative acts. Municipal authorities hold the authority to inspect and classify buildings as dangerous. Owners are notified to undertake necessary repairs or secure their properties, with demolition a potential option in cases of imminent risk. Both Maharashtra and Delhi enforce mandatory structural evaluations for older buildings.

title: DGCA's new eGCA platform aims to make aviation incident reporting faster
content: The DGCA is developing a digital platform for faster aviation incident reporting. This initiative aims to enhance the country's aviation safety oversight capabilities. Airfare pricing is determined by dynamic supply and demand forces. The government generally avoids regulating airfares to maintain market competitiveness. However, it intervenes in exceptional circumstances to ensure fair pricing.

title: Colby Covington targets ex-UFC middleweight champ after Belal Muhammad clash
content: The scheduled wrestling match between Colby Covington and Belal Muhammad is no longer taking place. After weeks of rumors following their heated brawl at Real American Freestyle (RAF) 11, Covington has now confirmed that the matchup has been called off. Speaking to the Submission Radio, former UFC interim welterweight champion has revealed that he is now looking at different opponents for his next fight.

title: 12 workers trapped in Sikkim tunnel after landslide blocks entrance
content: Twelve workers are trapped inside a Sikkim tunnel after a landslide blocked its entrance. A suspected gas leak inside the Samardung tunnel complicated ongoing rescue operations. Rescue teams are equipped with gas masks to reach the trapped individuals. Ambulances are on standby as efforts continue to free the workers. The tunnel is part of a highway construction project in Namchi district.

title: TheMightyBA's Streamer University 2026 controversy explained
content: The controversy at Streamer University 2026 surrounding TheMightyBA intensified when footage surfaced of him patting MeesterKeem's head, triggering a storm of criticism. Ludwig stood by TheMightyBA, suggesting that redemption is possible, drawing parallels to IShowSpeed’s remarkable evolution. With persistent effort and time, creators can address their past errors and forge a path to redemption.

title: 'No case': HC rejects plea of Mamata faction to operate TMC accounts worth Rs 440cr
content: The Calcutta high court denied interim relief to the Trinamool Congress faction. Three party bank accounts frozen by the Enforcement Directorate will remain inaccessible. The court found no prima facie case for the petitioners' request. The Enforcement Directorate is investigating alleged money laundering and dishonest financial transactions. The matter will be heard again on August 26.

title: Spain's Rodri scripts history, joins football's most exclusive club after WC win
content: Rodri achieved a rare football honour by winning the World Cup 2026 with Spain. He joined legends Gerd Müller, Franz Beckenbauer, and Zinedine Zidane with this achievement. Rodri also secured the World Cup Golden Ball as the tournament's best player. His exceptional passing and defensive contributions were vital for Spain's victory. This win cements Rodri's status among football's all-time great midfielders.

title: ‘Avengers: Doomsday’ trailer out: RDJ’s Doctor Doom steals the spotlight
content: The highly anticipated Avengers: Doomsday trailer has been officially released by the Russo Brothers.  This new installment features the Fantastic Four and original Avengers preparing for battle.  Thor rallies the heroes, emphasizing the importance of unity against a formidable threat.  The trailer showcases intense action and emotional storytelling, quickly becoming a fan favorite.  Avengers: Doomsday is scheduled for a December 18, 2026 release date.

title: World’s top 10 costliest rental markets: Where do Mumbai and Delhi rank
content: Around the globe, rental prices fluctuate widely, showcasing vast disparities among major cities. New York and Zurich top the charts, being the priciest for rentals. In contrast, Mumbai and Bengaluru emerge as affordable options by global standards. When it comes to property purchases, Hong Kong reigns as the most expensive. However, the overall appeal and affordability of a city transcend just housing costs.

title: Ashwin points to selection bias, explains why Rohit and Virat are 'impossible to drop'
content: Ravichandran Ashwin stated dropping Rohit Sharma and Virat Kohli is nearly impossible. Their extraordinary achievements and immense fan following make them undroppable players. Ashwin highlighted the disparity in how batters and bowlers are perceived in Indian cricket. Bowlers receive less public backing and face limited criticism when dropped. This contrasts sharply with the potential uproar from dropping batting icons.

title: 'Got no commitments': CJP leaders handover 3 demands to JP Nadda; minister says end 'sit-in'
content: Cockroach Janata Party leaders met Union minister JP Nadda regarding the NEET-UG paper leak case. Protesters marched towards Parliament demanding accountability and specific government actions. The Union minister sought time to consult leadership on the party's demands. Meanwhile, Union Home Minister Amit Shah met the Education Minister at Parliament. Police used mild force and tear gas as demonstrators attempted to breach barricades.

title: WATCH: Spain players form guard of honour for Messi after World Cup win
content: Spain secured the FIFA World Cup 2026 title after a hard-fought extra-time victory over Argentina.  The Spanish team formed a guard of honour for Lionel Messi and his teammates.  Ferran Torres scored the decisive goal which handed La Roja their second World Cup.  Messi's quest for a second World Cup title ended as Argentina's reign concluded.  Spain's disciplined defence successfully contained the legendary Argentine captain throughout the match.

title: Europe's extreme heat is threatening Italy's 800-year-old Parmesan tradition
content: Europe faces increasingly severe heatwaves, impacting infrastructure and public health significantly.  Temperatures are rising nearly twice the global average, causing widespread concern.  This extreme heat directly affects milk production for Parmigiano Reggiano cheese.  Farmers struggle to maintain cow health and feed supplies due to prolonged heat.  The vital Italian cheese industry faces economic and food security challenges.

title: 'Maaro sir': Video of protester standing firm amid Delhi Police crackdown goes viral
content: A protester stood before police during a Cockroach Janta Party march in Delhi.  He repeatedly asked officers to beat him while remaining in place.  Police used lathis and tear gas to disperse the large crowd of demonstrators.  Many protesters dispersed, but this man continued to stand his ground.  Security forces stopped the marchers at multiple points across central Delhi.

title: Despite Trump-Xi deal, China's rare earth magnet exports to US remain low
content: China's commitment to maintain supplies of critical materials, including rare-earth magnets, formed a central element of the October agreement under which Trump and Chinese President Xi Jinping agreed to pause the trade war.

title: 'Weaponisation of water': Pak seeks Canada's help to revive Indus Waters Treaty with India
content: Pakistan's Deputy Prime Minister Ishaq Dar requested Canadian support for the Indus Waters Treaty.  He stated India suspended the agreement, adding a new dynamic to security.  Dar urged India to end water weaponization and uphold international law.  India maintains its position remains in abeyance due to terrorism.  Pakistan views the Indus as a lifeline and will defend its sovereignty.

title: Hema Malini breaks down, reveals Dharmendra never stopped her from working after marriage
content: Hema Malini tearfully recalled her late husband Dharmendra's unwavering support and encouragement. She expressed gratitude for his presence throughout her personal and professional life.  Her daughters Esha and Ahana shared touching tributes, expressing pride and love.  Ahana praised her mother's resilience and the values instilled by both parents. Esha affirmed her support, calling Hema Malini a strong woman and inspiration.

title: 'He has shut up everybody': Ravi Shastri defends Rohit after Lord's century
content: Ravi Shastri supports Rohit Sharma, urging management to let him play as long as desired. Sharma silenced critics with a historic century at Lord's in the third ODI. He stated external noise does not affect his focus on team success. Sharma's performance came amid speculation about his future international career. The veteran opener continues to represent India and contribute to team achievements.

title: Delhi HC seeks comprehensive medical record on Sonam Wangchuk's condition
content: The Delhi High Court requested comprehensive medical records for Sonam Wangchuk. Authorities must submit all health documents and reports from various hospitals. Doctors treating Wangchuk were directed to appear before the court. This action follows an appeal against a single judge's decision on his hospital transfer. The court is reviewing the government's decision regarding his deteriorating health condition.

title: 'His retirement will be decided': Mohammad Kaif makes bold statement on Rohit Sharma's future after Lord's heroics
content: Mohammad Kaif backs Rohit Sharma amid speculation about his international cricket future.  Kaif insists retirement is a personal choice, not dictated by public opinion.  Rohit recently scored a historic century at Lord's, silencing critics effectively.  He believes team management support would greatly benefit the veteran opener.  Kaif hopes for an end to unnecessary noise, allowing Rohit to play peacefully.

title: Ram Temple donation row: Trust deploys new cash-counting team, two-shift system
content: The Ram Temple trust has implemented a new cash counting system with two shifts.  This new process is now under the supervision of the State Bank of India.  A new in-charge has been appointed after the previous one was jailed.  This follows an investigation into alleged theft of donation money.  Authorities arrested eight individuals and recovered significant cash and valuables.

title: Messi branded a ‘baby’ after desperate attempt to get Cucurella sent off
content: Messi’s World Cup final frustration sparked controversy after he appealed for action against Marc Cucurella’s mouth-covering exchange during Argentina’s defeat to Spain. While no punishment followed, pundits criticised the reaction as unsportsmanlike. Spain ultimately prevailed through Ferran Torres’ extra-time winner, ending Argentina’s title defence and leaving Messi’s final World Cup appearance marked by disappointment.

title: Quote of the day by Sylvester Stallone: ‘If you know what you're worth...'
content: Sylvester Stallone reminds us that true success is rooted in resilience rather than blind confidence. He advocates for embracing failures while holding firm to our goals. Life’s myriad challenges often test our resolve, but many great accomplishments arise only after we have weathered numerous setbacks and obstacles. Ultimately, it's how we respond to adversity that shapes the path toward achieving our dreams.

title: Gujarat Housing Board’s 100% penalty waiver scheme: Eligibility & how to apply
content: Gujarat's 100% Penalty Mafi Yojana offers a complete waiver of late payment penalties. Beneficiaries of Gujarat Housing Board and Slum Clearance Cell can apply. Applicants must pay all pending arrears in a single lump sum by March 31. This scheme helps beneficiaries clear dues and move towards property ownership. Online applications are now being accepted for this beneficial initiative.

title: Special court in Mumbai sentences 44 Somalian pirates to life imprisonment; praises Navy for prompt action
content: In March 2024, a hijacking alert was received from Iranian flagged FV Al Kambar, leading to the arrest of nine Somalian nationals after INS Trushul and Sumedha responded

title: Fishers allege Mamallan reservoir plan ignores tidal inundation risks
content: They argued that the WRD’s proposal and the technical assessment by the IIT-M, had not adequately assessed this risk; they further said that converting the marsh into a reservoir would eliminate about 4,375 acres of natural flood storage, increasing flood risk in nearby areas

title: State clears ₹522 crore power works for 2027 Godavari Pushkaralu
content: RDSS contracts extended till March 2027; ₹177 crore SCADA upgrade also cleared to strengthen power distribution network ahead of the mega religious event.

title: Nellore children’s park to feature zip line, cycling track soon, says Minister
content: Addressing a review meeting, Ponguru Narayana says all parks in the city will be modernised in a phased manner to meet the needs of the public

title: Rajya Sabha adjourned six times over paper leak
content: Opposition shouts slogans when three members who switched from the Trinamool to the BJP took oath; Congress members heckle BJP leader Mahesh Kewat, the member from Madhya Pradesh, and shout “seat chori”

title: Centre introduces Bill to raise strength of judges in Supreme Court to 38
content: The Bill seeks to replace an ordinance promulgated by the President in May to increase the strength of judges in the top court from 34 to 38, including the Chief Justice of India

title: Stake sale at Vizhinjam: empowered panel may take 3-4 months to submit its report
content: The committee, headed by the Chief Secretary, has yet to examine the nuances of the stake sale or complete the exercise

title: Pothundy double murder case: a death penalty verdict with rare focus on rehabilitating victims’ family
content: Court directs District Legal Services Authority, Palakkad, to initiate rehabilitation measures for orphaned children of the victims; legal experts say such a specific direction was rare in criminal judgments

title: Faith and its fallout
content: In Ayodhya, weeks after allegations of money being siphoned off from donations at the Ram temple surfaced, pilgrims and residents say it has triggered a crisis of trust. Devotees say their faith in Lord Ram and the temple remains intact, but many have grown cautious about donating. Locals report a dip in visitors, though they attribute part of that to seasonal factors as well. As an SIT probe seeks accountability, Ishita Mishra reports from the temple town, where faith, transparency, and the city’s religious tourism hopes hang in the balance

title: Andhra and Telangana direct tax collections grow 19.47% in first quarter of 2026-27
content: Principal Chief Commissioner of Income Tax said the region’s net collection reached ₹41,568 crore up to July 17; Andhra Pradesh recorded 17% growth at ₹6,607 crore

title: Rajya Sabha MP Manoj Jha urges Modi to prevent demolition of Mohammad Ali Jauhar University buildings in Rampur
content: “The proposed action is not only grossly excessive, but also appears to be politically vengeful and communally motivated,” says the Rajya Sabha member

title: Delhi HC to hear Wangchuk hospital transfer plea on July 21
content: Bench seeks medical records and doctors’ presence; counsel says Wangchuk has a constitutional right to continue his protest; Solicitor-General says any deterioration in his health or any unforeseen event can have repercussions

title: HC strikes down Kerala Vesting of Minerals Act as unconstitutional
content: The legislation, enacted in 2021, vested all mineral rights in the Malabar region with the State government. The landowners moved the court which ruled that State legislature is not qualified to enact the law

title: Minister urges daily monitoring for El Nino preparedness in Anantapur
content: Participating in a meeting, Keshav directs that a liaison officer be appointed from the district to coordinate with senior officials in the State capital regarding these conditions

title: Accurate disaster prediction key to saving lives, says R.P. Sisodiya
content: Delivering a lecture on disaster preparedness and response, the Special Chief Secretary to Government, Industries and Commerce (Handlooms and Textiles) calls for close cooperation among the Centre, State government, district administration and line departments during emergencies

title: Don’t surrender Thekkinkadu Maidan to communal forces, says CPI
content: Seeks all-party meeting to preserve Maidan as a public space; poet K.G. Sankara Pillai warns against shrinking democratic spaces

title: Sonam Wangchuk to continue fast, cites police action against protesters
content: Mt. Wangchuk said he is "touched and moved" by the manner in which the protesters have maintained peace despite "provocation"

title: TMC used 1993 police firing for political gains, hid inquiry report: CM after tabling panel findings
content: The July 21 incident was used politically; many people have built their political and personal fortunes on it, said West Bengal Chief Minister Suvendu Adhikari

title: YSRCP alleges deterioration in law and order in Andhra Pradesh
content: Perni Nani accuses the TDP-led NDA government of weakening the police system through political interference and cites the Guntur assault case

title: Guntur civic body cancels private commercial waste collection contracts
content: Corporation assumes direct responsibility of commercial waste collection; Commissioner clarifies that traders no longer need to pay any user fee for waste collection under the new arrangement

title: Church does not receive any foreign funds in personal accounts, KCBC
content: Catholic Church leadership in Kerala reiterates concerns over Foreign Contribution (Regulation) Amendment Bill, 2026

title: Registration Service Kendras intended to help public, not privatise services, says Naidu
content: RSKs provide more citizen-friendly services and do not amount to ‘privatisation’ of Sub-Registrar Offices, they function on the lines of Passport Seva Kendras, asserts Chief Minister Chandrababu Naidu; he directs officials to explain facts to the public and highlight benefits of the new system

title: CM seeks Centre’s support for aquaculture, tobacco sectors in Andhra Pradesh
content: Chandrababu Naidu urges Union Finance Minister to reduce GST on aerators from 18% to 5%, and grant aquaculture farmers a one-time additional ₹5 lakh credit limit under the Kisan Credit Card scheme

title: Final report on Sabarimala gold ‘theft’ will be filed without delay, SIT tells Kerala HC
content: ADGP’s report says final opinion of the lab provides an explanation of the process allegedly adopted by the accused in committing the offence

title: YSRCP women leaders seek Home Minister's resignation over Guntur assault
content: They announce Statewide protests at Ambedkar statues on July 21 , allege that women are becoming increasingly vulnerable under the current government

title: Kerala govt. hikes rubber support price from ₹200 to ₹250 per kg
content: Farmers and rubber producer organisations had repeatedly urged the State government to hike the support price as production costs had increased

content: The Defence desk covers wars, weapons, militaries and strategy across the world. From border tensions and defence technology to global power rivalries, it explains the security stories shaping countries, conflicts and the future of warfare. Read More

title: "I Will Continue My Fast": Sonam Wangchuk After Government Meets Protesters
content: Wangchuk's wife, Gitanjali Angmo, shared on social media a handwritten message from her husband that offered solidarity to the protesters under the banner of the Cockroach Janta Party (CJP)

title: "Tear-Gassing, Lathicharging Students Not India's Way": Rahul Gandhi On CJP March
content: The Delhi Police fired tear gas and baton-charged protesters on Monday as they tried to march on parliament to demand the resignation of Education Minister Dharmendra Pradhan over irregularities in major examinations, including question paper leaks and technical glitches.

title: RBI Flags Conflict, Monsoon Risks. These May Already Be Pushing Inflation
content: The RBI has already revised its inflation outlook upward. In its June monetary policy, the central bank raised its FY27 CPI inflation forecast to 5.1 per cent from 4.6 per cent projected earlier.

title: Opinion: Opinion | Not Just Jets, China Is Developing A '5-Piece' Carrier Set, And India Should Worry
content: China is now the only state serially producing two distinct stealth fighter families simultaneously, with some estimates projecting that advanced-fighter output across all jet programmes could reach 300-400 aircraft annually by 2027.

title: US Says Cuba Infiltrated "Highest Reaches" Of American Government
content: A US State Department report alleged that Cuba's decades-long espionage campaign was "one of the most durable and damaging foreign intelligence penetrations in American history."

title: CJP Protest: "End Sit-In": Centre's Appeal After "Cordial Meeting" With CJP Leaders
content: "I have requested all the protesters to end their sit-in and assist the administration in restoring normalcy," Union minister JP Nadda said

title: 2 Students Killed After Rath Yatra Chariot Touches Live Wire In Jharkhand
content: The chariot came in contact with a live 11,000-volt overhead power line, injuring at least seven boys.

title: "Eye For An Eye": Houthis Declare Maritime Blockade On Saudi Arabia
content: The Houthis fired missiles at an airport in the southern Saudi Arabian city of Abha last Monday, after the Yemeni government said it hit Sanaa airport to block a flight from Iran with a Houthi delegation aboard it.

title: "A Time Bomb": Residents Fear Ghazipur-Like Landfill In Faridabad
content: The biggest fear that clouds the minds of the citizens is whether this would mark the inauguration of Faridabad's version of a Ghazipur landfill.

title: Headless Since Yahya Sinwar's Death 2 Years Ago, Hamas Gets New Chief
content: Born in 1960, Hayya survived an Israeli assassination attempt in Doha last year, though one of his sons was killed in the strike.

title: CJP Parliament March LIVE Updates: "Protests To Continue," Says CJP After Meeting With JP Nadda
content: CJP Parliament March LIVE Updates: The police had denied the Cockroach Janta Party the permission to hold the protest march and banned large gatherings.

title: Political Row Erupts In Bihar Over Saffron Colour Plan For 500 New Model Schools
content: Chief Minister Samrat Choudhary on Sunday inaugurated 551 model schools, and according to sources in the education department, officials have been directed to paint all the Saraswati Vidya Niketan schools saffron instead of conventional colours such as pink, sky blue or yellow.

title: Opinion: Opinion | The Refugee With No Passport, And The Illegal Immigrant With Indian Papers
content: How can you expect a passport from a refugee? That's the question - posed by the Delhi High Court - that now defines the story of Maung Henry Htoo Aung Linn.

title: Tear Gas, Lathicharge As At Least 20,000 March With CJP To Parliament
content: CJP Parliament March: The Cockroach Janta Party (CJP) supporters had announced a "Chalo Sansad" march against alleged irregularities in NEET.

title: "Violent, Aggressive": China Coast Guard Attack On Filipino Sailor Caught On Camera
content: The assault was captured on video by other Filipino sailors in the hotly disputed Second Thomas Shoal and set off angry condemnations by Philippine officials.

title: Op Sindoor Big Success, Pak Army Tried To Hide Failure: Imran Khan's Sister
content: Pakistan's National Cyber Crime Investigation Agency (NCCIA) has summoned Noreen Niazi, sister of former prime minister Imran Khan

title: Iran Is In "Full-Scale War" With US, Says President Pezeshkian
content: "The most important battlefield today is the economy and the livelihoods of the people," said the Iranian president in a meeting in Tehran with judicial officials.

title: 'Politicians Come And Go': Larry The Cat Welcomes Another UK PM, His 7th
content: Recruited to deal with a rodent problem, Larry arrived at the prime minister's official residence on February 15, 2011, when David Cameron was leader.

title: "Are Surveillance Norms Being Followed?" High Court To Delhi Cops On CJP Protest
content: "At Jantar Mantar, every protest is always recorded... There is no surveillance," Solicitor General Tushar Mehta argued.

title: Class 12 Student Shot Dead On Way To School In Bihar's Samastipur
content: The police said bike-borne criminals shot dead Umesh Thakur's daughter, Sonam Kumari. She was a student of Kasturba Vidyalaya, Pusa Road.

content: A digital news desk journalist at The Times of India with over two years of experience. Closely tracks foreign affairs and international politics. Fondness for precise language and clever headlines. When not editing news, he is usually refining a turn of phrase. Read More

title: Trump scores own-goal as World Cup overflows with politics
content: 80,000-plus spectators greeted him with lusty boos as he strode onto the field for the trophy presentation . supporters generated enough cheers to produce enough cheer to produce the trophy . a u.s. president has remained planted centre stage like an enthusiastic wedding guest .

title: Ram temple donation 'theft' row: Simple case of crime, don't politicise it, says SC
